title: Trump reestablishes naval blockade on Iran and demands a 20% toll in the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Hostilities between the United States and Iran intensify with mutual attacks and the declaration of a new blockade.

title: Parliament approves reform of the imperial succession law with agreement between government and opposition
summary: The opposition proposes to remove the restriction of succession to males from the former imperial family.

title: 44-year-old man arrested for stabbing four people in the city of Saiki, Oita
summary: The attack occurred on a public street; the suspect was arrested for possession of a knife.

title: Secondary capital project in Osaka remains without a vote and the government considers extending the legislative period
summary: The initiative promoted by the prime minister fails to reach consensus and its discussion in parliament is postponed.

title: Parliament approves creation of the Disaster Management Agency as a central emergency response body
summary: The new agency, which will start operating in November, will coordinate measures against major natural disasters.

title: Supreme Court recognizes three citizens as legitimate parties in the lawsuit over the relocation of the Henoko base
summary: The ruling allows the plaintiffs to continue the judicial process against the construction of a new base in Okinawa.

title: Kabuki actor Nakamura Kosaburo under investigation for alleged hit-and-run offense
summary: Authorities question the actor over an incident in which a pedestrian was injured.

title: EU presents plan to limit social media use for children under 13 and regulate smartphone access
summary: The European Commission seeks to protect children from digital risks through new restrictions.

title: Heat wave pushes temperatures above 38 degrees in Japan and activates heatstroke alerts in 14 prefectures
summary: Authorities recommend caution as cases of heatstroke rise.

title: Global stock markets suffer losses due to rising tensions in the Middle East
summary: The Nikkei index falls more than 1,300 points and the yen rises against the dollar due to risk aversion.

title: Fire at a bar in Bangkok kills 28, most of them in the restrooms
summary: Authorities investigate the cause of the fire, with no Japanese victims.

title: Supreme Court confirms illegality of censorship of death row inmate's letters to his lawyer
summary: The state must compensate the inmate for violating the right to defense.

title: Bankruptcy of Zentoshin financial firm leaves bad loans worth over 46.5 billion yen across 21 financial institutions
summary: The collapse of the credit cooperative affects numerous regional banks.

title: Actor Sam Neill, Dr. Grant from 'Jurassic Park', dies at 78
summary: The New Zealand performer leaves a long career in film and television.

title: Makoto Usui, composer of the chorus song symbolizing Kobe earthquake reconstruction, dies at 65
summary: The song 'Shiawase Hakoberu You ni' is an emblem of recovery after the 1995 earthquake.

title: Prosecutors reopen case against NHK party leader for threats to a lawmaker in Hyogo
summary: The review committee considered the previous decision not to prosecute as unjustified.

title: Zelensky relieves Ukrainian prime minister and plans to appoint him ambassador to the United States
summary: The change seeks to strengthen relations with Washington amid the war with Russia.

title: Japan approves requirement to label AI-generated content in election campaigns
summary: The new regulation aims to curb misinformation on social media during elections.

title: Sacred child of Kyoto's Gion Festival rides white horse in opening ceremony
summary: Kyoto's annual festival begins with traditional rites dating back centuries.

title: Government and opposition reach agreement on tax credit for low-income workers
summary: The system will be expanded to also cover people with illnesses or disabilities.
